title: Mandator-class Star Dreadnaught

The '''''Mandator''-class Star Dreadnaught''' was a [[Warship/Legends|warship]]<ref name="Warfare" /> developed by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]]<ref name="AOTC" /> two decades before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] as the centerpiece of the [[Kuat sector/Legends|Kuat sector's]] defense [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. A humpbacked warship like its predecessor, the [[Procurator-class Star Battlecruiser|''Procurator''-class Star Battlecruiser]], it was the first [[Starship/Legends|starship]] to receive the designation of [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Dreadnaught]] by the [[Republic Navy War College/Legends|Anaxes War College]]. Regulations from the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformation]] limited the ''Mandator''-class's speed and armament, though they were still formidable warships.<ref name="Warfare" /> The ''Mandator''-class's ventral protrusion was the result of its large reactor.<ref name="AOTC" /> By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the Clone Wars, seven Star Dreadnaughts were in service—three in the Kuat sector, and one in each of the [[Azure sector/Legends|Azure]], [[Ixtlar sector/Legends|Ixtlar]], [[Alsaka sector/Legends|Alsaka]], and [[Humbarine sector/Legends|Humbarine sectors]]. Its successor, the [[Mandator II-class Star Dreadnaught/Legends|''Mandator II''-class Star Dreadnaught]], featured heavier armor, better weaponry, and better [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] capabilities. The ''Mandator II''-class was unveiled during the Clone Wars, which also saw three of the original ''Mandator''-class ships refitted for combat.<ref name="Warfare" />
